programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: Medical toxicologist, Dr. Charles O'Connell gave a summary on Tom Merriman's poor health in the lead up to his death. It included an enlarged liver, congestive heart failure and underlying lung problems. This was in conjuction to his alleged alcohol and prescription drug abuse. Next up, Jade Janks took the stand in her own murder trial. It was her attempt to clear herself from the first-degree murder in the death of her stepfather.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: Prosecutors kept bringing in witnesses who had been at the parade when an SUV came crashing into the crowd. The State's latest witness was a woman who had been hit, along with her 9-year-old daughter.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: A survivor is cross-examined by the same man accused of hitting her and her daughter with his car.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: Traumatized and grieving survivors, including two youth baseball coaches, take the witness stand.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: A dance team coach took the stand in the deadly parade crash trial and gave a harrowing account of how a red SUV hit many of the dancers in her group. They were among the sixty-one people injured. In the end, six people died.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: A dance coach breaks down remembering the moment she and her team faced the oncoming car.

programme.title: Interview With a Killer
programme.desc: David Scott challenges Halford on her role that fateful night and holds her accountable.

programme.title: Interview With a Killer
programme.desc: Delgadillo explains how a thousand-dollar robbery of crystal meth kicked off an evening of mayhem.

programme.title: Deadly Daughter Murder Trial
programme.desc: Opening statements began in the murder case involving a 14 year old who pulled the trigger and shot and killed her mother, Ashley Smylie. She also was accused of shooting her stepdad, who survived the attack. Carly Gregg faces murder, attempted murder, and tampering with evidence charges. Her defense claimed it was mental illness and not a premeditated plan that led to the murder. But ultimately, it would be up to the jury to decide whether Carly Gregg was responsible.

programme.title: Deadly Daughter Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors listened to the harrowing 911 call and then watched police bodycam video from the first responding officer. Prosecutors told the jury that after Carly Gregg shot and killed her mom, she lured her stepdad home via a text. When Heath Smylie arrived, the State alleged the defendant shot him in the shoulder before he could wrestle the gun away from her.

programme.title: Deadly Daughter Murder Trial
programme.desc: Carly Gregg's defense team had questions for the first officer who arrived at her home after her mother, Ashley Smylie was killed. By the time police got there, the defendant had run away but was later caught and taken into custody. Next to the stand was the defendant's stepfather and the victim's husband, Heath Smylie. Prosecutors alleged Gregg also wanted to have her stepfather killed but her defense alleged she slipped into a manic, mental health crisis.

programme.title: Deadly Daughter Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors learned more about the defendant's background which included secrets she kept from her parents. On the stand was Carly Gregg's stepfather, Heath Smylie, who was the other victim in the case. He told jurors about finding Ashley's body and then Carly shooting at him. Gregg was 14 years old at the time she killed her mother and wounded her stepfather. Prosecutors tried her as an adult in the crimes.

programme.title: Deadly Daughter Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: She Was Out Of It That Day
programme.desc: The cross-examination began on Carly Gregg's stepfather. Heath Smylie told jurors about the traumatic moment when he came across his wife, Ashley, on the ground after she was killed and then about his own life, which was suddenly threatened when he got home. Gregg's stepfather was convinced that she didn't recognize him at all when he came home that day. He said she appeared frightened and was screaming just before she pulled the trigger.

programme.title: Judgment With Ashleigh Banfield
programme.desc: The story that 34 year-old married schoolteacher Mary Kay Letourneau was having sex with a student.

programme.title: Judgment With Ashleigh Banfield
programme.desc: Reverend Randall Foos hit and killed 19-year-old Sara Casey as she rode her bicycle.

programme.title: Judgment With Ashleigh Banfield
programme.desc: Haq shot seven women; the jury has to decide if his mental health issues make it less of a crime.

programme.title: Judgment With Ashleigh Banfield
programme.desc: When baby Ava became ill, her parents insisted the power of prayer was the only cure she needed.

programme.title: Judgment With Ashleigh Banfield
programme.desc: Neil Beagley's parents are charged with criminally negligent homicide.

programme.title: Taboo Photos Murder Trial
programme.desc: Jurors learned more about Jade Janks relationship with Tom Merriman in the years leading up to his death. Janks told jurors that he had no issues with her stepdad and the two had a close relationship. She would often call him Dad. Both defendant and victim in this case was on a number of different prescription medication. This would be an important aspect to Merriman's demise since he died of an apparent overdose of a sleeping drug.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.desc: Michelle Wyatt is found strangled to death in 1980. Few leads arise until 41 years later, thanks to forensics.

programme.desc: In 1976, Leslie Penrod Harris storms out of a restaurant after an argument with her husband. She never returns. Hours later, her naked body is found near El Toro Air Base twenty miles away.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After a young woman went missing, police suspected that her well-connected, married ex-lover was responsible.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1984, a couple swept through six states like a plague. They targeted adults and young girls for kidnap and murder. The gruesome crime spree left eight people dead, so the FBI launched a manhunt for killers Alton Coleman and Debra Denise Brown.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.sub-title: The First and Kennedy Street Crew
programme.desc: Powerful street gangs in Washington D.C. enforced their power with deadly violence. The gang leader had fled to Africa, a hunt that ended in the 1994 Washington D.C. Metropolitan Police Headquarters shootout.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves in the Alaskan wilderness. In one of the first cases of successful criminal investigative analysis, FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind and captured the serial killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A sniper murdered two Black men as they jogged with two white women. The FBI placed their suspect on their Ten Most Wanted list and began an intense manhunt. What followed was the arrest, trial and conviction of a white supremacist serial killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1986, a psychotic killer shot and killed a police officer at point-blank range, leading investigators in a wild chase across the Midwest. As his tally of murders and abductions grew, the FBI raced against time to put a stop to the deranged gunman.

programme.desc: Over a 25-year career of murder, Richard Biegenwald killed at least six people, but he is suspected of many more murders. Imprisoned in 1958 aged just 18 for shooting dead a grocery store owner, Biegenwald was released 17 years later.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In October 2004, Rachel O'Reilly left her home to drop her children off at school. Less than 20 minutes after returning home she was murdered. Her husband, Joe O'Reilly, staged the crime scene to make it look like a burglary gone wrong.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.desc: A driver said he couldn't have hit and killed a pedestrian because his Jeep had been sold months ago.

programme.desc: A young woman attends evening church services... then disappears. When her abandoned car is found, the tank is empty and a gas can she kept in her trunk for emergencies is missing.

programme.desc: A serial killer was on the loose and police had to find him before he struck again. Their most promising lead was an unusual one: a bloody fingerprint on the body of one of the victims.

programme.desc: When a woman went missing, friends and family were determined to find her. Their worst fears were confirmed weeks later when her body was discovered.

programme.desc: Volunteers and police spend weeks searching for a missing girl. A psychic later leads them to a field where the body was found.

programme.desc: A man's wife took her own life and his college sweetheart killed herself the same way. Was it coincidence?

programme.desc: Detectives search for the bombers of two churches in Illinois, hoping that the materials used in the remnants of the handmade bombs will offer the clues to catch the culprits.
